description_long: A group of three coconut palm trees is depicted, each with a tall, cylindrical trunk and a crown of long, arching, feather-like fronds. The trunks are textured with a rough, fibrous bark that appears grayish-brown, and the fronds are a deep green with visible segmentation. The largest palm stands centrally, flanked by two smaller palms, creating a natural cluster. The fronds radiate outward in all directions, with some overlapping and casting shadows on the trunks below. No individual coconuts are distinctly visible among the fronds, but the overall form and foliage are characteristic of coconut palms. The asset is presented against a plain white background, with no environmental context.
description: A cluster of three coconut palm trees with rough, grayish trunks and lush, arching green fronds. The palms vary in size and are grouped closely together.
description_view_0: Three coconut palms are visible, with the tallest in the center and two smaller ones in front and to the left, showing the dense arrangement of fronds.
description_view_1: The palms are viewed from a slight angle, highlighting the overlapping fronds and the staggered positioning of the trunks.
description_view_2: The central palm is prominent, with the two smaller palms clearly visible to the left and right, emphasizing the cluster formation.
description_view_3: The largest palm dominates the view, with one smaller palm in the foreground and another partially obscured behind, showing the layered frond structure.
attribute_husk_pattern: The trunks display a vertically striated, fibrous texture with irregular, rough markings and a dense, linear arrangement of fibers running parallel to the trunk. The fronds have a segmented, feathery appearance with fine, linear veins radiating outward.
attribute_growth_cluster: cluster of three; the palms are grouped closely together with their trunks emerging from the same general area.
attribute_proximity_to_palm_trees: The coconuts are not individually visible, but the asset consists of three palm trees in immediate proximity, forming a tight cluster.
